title: Minimum Cost to Reach the End of the Road

statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.
statement.inputFormat: Input will consist of an integer n representing the number of houses and a string of spaces separated integers representing the cost to reach each house. The houses will be numbered from 1 to n and costs will be in non-decreasing order.
statement.outputFormat: Output the minimum cost to reach the end of the road.
statement.notes: Time complexity should not exceed O(nlogn).

constraints.description: List of non-decreasing costs to reach each house

examples.explanation: The minimum cost to reach the end of the road is 20, which can be obtained by choosing the 4th and 5th houses.
The minimum cost to reach the end of the road is 10.

hints.text: Use dynamic programming to keep track of the minimum cost to reach each house.

editorial.title: Minimum Cost to Reach the End of the Road
editorial.intuition: The correct approach is to use dynamic programming to keep track of the minimum cost to reach each house.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach: Try every possible path to reach the end of the road.
editorial.betterSolution: Better solution: Use dynamic programming to keep track of the minimum cost to reach each house.
editorial.optimalSolution: Optimal solution: Use dynamic programming and binary search to find the minimum cost to reach the end of the road.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correctness of the optimal solution can be proved using mathematical induction.
editorial.dryRun: Example run of the optimal solution.

functionSignature.cpp: long long minimumCost(int n, vector<int>& costs);
functionSignature.python: def minimumCost(self, n: int, costs: list[int]) -> int:
